mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and surges in consumer price inflation indicate stagflationary supply shocks that compress profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle contraction phases, a sequential drop in housing starts and real GDP growth signals a broad-based demand collapse that systematically depresses corporate revenue expectations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 40% of late-cycle contraction episodes identified by the signal.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.0 percent, it marks a definitive shift into restrictive monetary policy that increases borrowing costs and reduces equity risk premiums, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of crossing events, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust aggregate supply, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising production periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth and rising consumer credit outstanding indicate synchronized labor and demand expansion, which fuels earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a quantitative tightening regime, a rapid month-over-month increase in the consumer price index alongside rising federal funds rates accelerates real rate expectations, compressing equity duration and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive or neutral in more than 45% of QT regime episodes identified by the signal, or the joint predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.
